## `pc archeologist --reset`

The "New" count is driven by the capture ledger in `~/.proactive-context/captured-sessions/` (571 markers), which is independent of `docs/wiki` — that's why deleting the wiki didn't reset anything. The new flag forgets those markers so sessions count as new again. It short-circuits the whole pipeline: no scan-for-work, no LLM, no picker. [...] Done. Here's what I added.

## `pc archeologist --reset`

The "New" count is driven by the capture ledger in `~/.proactive-context/captured-sessions/` (571 markers), which is independent of `docs/wiki` — that's why deleting the wiki didn't reset anything. The new flag forgets those markers so sessions count as new again. It short-circuits the whole pipeline: no scan-for-work, no LLM, no picker. [...] **For your case — full start-over (all projects):**
```bash
pc archeologist --reset
```
Removes `captured-sessions/`, plus transient `pending-captures/` and `session-locks/`. Prompts for confirmation first. [...] **Just one project:**
```bash
pc archeologist --reset --project proactive-context
```
Scans `~/.claude/projects/`, resolves that project's session IDs, and deletes only those markers. Uses the **same matching as a real run**, so reset scope == run scope (note: substring match — `proactive-context` matched 3 dirs / 56 sessions here, since it catches the worktrees too; pass the exact normalized cwd to narrow). [...] Scans `~/.claude/projects/`, resolves that project's session IDs, and deletes only those markers. Uses the **same matching as a real run**, so reset scope == run scope (note: substring match — `proactive-context` matched 3 dirs / 56 sessions here, since it catches the worktrees too; pass the exact normalized cwd to narrow). [...] - `--yes` skips the confirmation prompt (for reset, `--yes` means "don't ask" — not "mine all"; it's allowed alongside `--project`). [...] - Non-TTY without `--yes` **refuses** rather than wiping blind. [...] Assistant: Full reset and the non-TTY safety guard both work. Now verify the `--project` matching/counting path (against an isolated empty ledger so nothing real is touched):

Assistant: The `--project` + `--yes` mutual-exclusion check fires before reset. For reset, `--yes` means "skip the prompt", not "mine all" — so reset must short-circuit *before* that validation. Let me reorder: [...] - `--output-dir DIR` targets an isolated ledger (`DIR/captured-sessions/`) instead of the global tree. [...] **Caveat:** that ledger is global/flat — it resets **all 38 projects**, not just `proactive-context`. The markers carry no project info, so there's no clean per-project wipe from the marker dir alone. If you ever used `--out <dir>` for an isolated run, the ledger is instead at `<dir>/captured-sessions/` (`archeologist.rs:591`).
